incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 5: assumes all numbers fit in range, missing that exact values require checking ones place digits

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Check the ones place digits: 3, 1, 9, 6. Are these multiples of 5? Only multiples of 5 end in 0 or 5.

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 10: assumes larger scale always works, missing that exact values require checking ones place digits

steps.text: Here's a hint: look at the ones place of each value. 23, 31, 9, 16. To show these exactly, your scale must count by a number that gets you to 3, 1, 9, and 6.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Ones place digits (3, 1, 9, 6) pulse to show they are not multiples of 5 or 10

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. The values are 23, 31, 9, 16. Look at the ones place: 3, 1, 9, 6. None end in 0 or 5, so they aren't multiples of 5 or 10. Scale of 2 counts by 2 and can show all these values exactly.
